'''Brodie Croft''' (born 14 July 1997) is an Australian professional [[rugby league]] footballer who plays as a {{rlp|so}} for the [[Leeds Rhinos]] in the [[Super League]]. 

He previously played for [[Salford Red Devils]] in the Super League, with whom he won the [[Man of Steel Award]] and the [[Albert Goldthorpe Medal]], and [[Melbourne Storm]] with whom he won the [[2018 World Club Challenge]] and the [[Brisbane Broncos]] as a {{rlp|sh}} in the [[National Rugby League|NRL]].

==Early life==
Croft was born in [[Dalby, Queensland]], Australia. He was educated at [[St. Joseph's College, Toowoomba]].<ref name="bronco">{{cite web | url=https://www.broncos.com.au/news/2019/12/03/career-path-brodie-croft/ | title=Career Path: Brodie Croft | publisher=broncos.com.au | date=3 December 2019 | accessdate=30 September 2024}}</ref>

He played his junior rugby league for the [[Highfields Eagles]] and was a member of the 2014 Churchie First XV alongside [[Kalyn Ponga]], and [[Jaydn Su'a]]<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.redsrugby.com.au/News/NewsArticles/tabid/581/ArticleID/10117/Queensland-Under-16-teams-announced.aspx|title=Queensland Under 16 teams announced|publisher=Redsrugby.com.au|date=|access-date=19 June 2016}}</ref> before being signed by the [[Melbourne Storm]].

He played for the [[Toowoomba Clydesdales]] in the [[Cyril Connell Cup]] during 2013, before moving to [[Brisbane]], to join the [[Eastern Suburbs Tigers]] in the [[Mal Meninga Cup]].<ref>{{cite web|author=NRL|url=http://www.qrl.com.au/news/2014/03/29/rd_5_cc_mm_scores.html|title=Rd 5 CC & MM scores|publisher=QRL|date=29 March 2014|access-date=19 June 2016}}</ref> The following year, he was named 18th man for the [[Queensland under-18 rugby league team]].<ref>{{cite web|author=NRL|url=http://www.qrl.com.au/news/2015/06/02/queensland_under_18_.html|title=Queensland Under 18 team|publisher=QRL|date=2 June 2015|access-date=19 June 2016}}</ref>

==Playing career==
===2016–2019: Melbourne Storm===
In 2016, Croft was named in the [[Queensland Academy of Sport|QAS]] under-20 squad and played for the Melbourne Storm's [[National Youth Competition (rugby league)|NYC]] team.<ref>{{cite web|author=Tim Costello and Josh Robertson|url=http://leagueunlimited.com/news/leagueunlimited-nyc-teams-2016-round-7/|title=LeagueUnlimited Holden Cup NYC Under 20's Teams - 2016 Round 7|publisher=League Unlimited|date=|access-date=18 June 2016}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|author=NRL|url=http://www.qrl.com.au/news/2016/01/06/broncos_players_domi.html|title=Broncos young guns dominate QAS U20s|publisher=QRL|date=6 January 2016|access-date=19 June 2016}}</ref> On 22 May, he made his [[Queensland Cup]] debut with Melbourne's feeder team, the Easts Tigers, scoring a try in their 52-20 win over the [[Ipswich Jets]].<ref>{{cite web|last=Crowhurst|first=Matt|url=http://www.qrl.com.au/news/2016/05/22/tigers_score_impress.html|title=Tigers score impressive win over Jets|publisher=QRL|date=22 May 2016|access-date=19 June 2016}}</ref>

In Round 15 of the [[2016 NRL season|2016 season]], he made his [[National Rugby League|NRL]] debut for the [[Melbourne Storm]] against [[St. George Illawarra Dragons|St. George Illawarra]].<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.nrl.com/updated-team-lists-dragons-v-storm/tabid/10874/newsid/98211/default.aspx|title=Updated Team lists: Dragons v Storm|publisher=NRL.com|date=18 June 2016|access-date=18 June 2016}}</ref> On 13 July, he represented the [[Queensland under-20 rugby league team]].<ref>{{cite web|author=NRL|url=http://www.qrl.com.au/news/2016/07/07/cowboy_salam_earns_l.html|title=Cowboy Salam earns late call-up|publisher=QRL|date=7 July 2016|access-date=15 July 2016}}</ref>

Croft started the [[2017 NRL season|2017 season]] by playing in the [[2017 NRL Auckland Nines|NRL Auckland Nines]]. He scored 3 tries and was named in the team of the tournament.<ref>{{cite web|author=Dan Walsh|url=http://www.heraldsun.com.au/sport/nrl/auckland-nines-2017-teens-and-tyros-headline-team-of-the-tournament/news-story/ed6fa4870af397b9098421b5d116e771|title=Auckland Nines 2017: Teens and tyros headline team of the tournament|publisher=Herald Sun|date=5 February 2017|access-date=5 February 2017}}</ref> In just his second NRL game, he slotted an extra-time field goal to secure victory over [[North Queensland Cowboys|North Queensland]] and in doing so, Melbourne escaped with a 23-22 win in golden-point.<ref>{{cite web|author=Travis Meyn|url=http://www.news.com.au/sport/breaking-news/storm-young-gun-brodie-crofts-field-goal-sinks-cowboys-2322-in-golden-point/news-story/b4a2777514d6a0cf363a240c520265d1|title=Storm young gun Brodie Croft's field goal sinks Cowboys 23-22 in golden point|publisher=news.com.au|date=17 June 2017|access-date=17 June 2017}}</ref> On 24 July, he signed a contract extension with the Storm, tying him to the club for at least another three years with a view to replacing [[Cooper Cronk]] in the long-term as Melbourne's halfback.  Croft was described by the media at the time as the next Cooper Cronk and was labelled a "Cooper Cronk clone".<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.smh.com.au/sport/nrl/melbourne-storm-sensation-brodie-croft-says-he-is-no-cooper-cronk-clone-20170617-gwt9o3.html/|title=Melbourne Storm sensation Brodie Croft says he is no Cooper Cronk clone|website=www.smh.com.au|date=17 June 2017}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|author=Dan Walsh|url=https://www.foxsports.com.au/nrl/nrl-premiership/teams/storm/nrl-2017-melbourne-storm-lock-in-big-three-successors-with-four-key-resignings/news-story/ba2f33ebd856b065be1370d2cc0fab53|title=NRL 2017: Melbourne Storm lock in Big Three successors with four key re-signings|work=Fox Sports|date=24 July 2017|access-date=26 July 2017}}</ref> On 19 August, with Cronk rested, Croft scored a hat-trick and had two try assists in a 44-12 win over the [[Newcastle Knights]] in just his fifth NRL game.<ref>{{cite web|author=Roy Ward|url=http://www.smh.com.au/rugby-league/league-match-report/melbourne-storm-claim-minor-premiership-as-brodie-croft-hattrick-inspires-win-20170819-gxzwal.html|title=Melbourne Storm claim minor premiership as Brodie Croft hat-trick inspires win|publisher=Sydney Morning Herald|date=19 August 2017|access-date=22 August 2017}}</ref> Despite this performance, he didn't feature in another NRL match for the remainder of the season due to the return of Cronk, leading the NRL side to the [[2017 NRL Grand Final]] victory.

In February 2018, Croft staked his claim as Melbourne's new halfback. He scored a try and three try assists in a composed performance against [[Leeds Rhinos|Leeds]] in the [[2018 World Club Challenge]] at [[AAMI Park]].<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.nrl.com/news/2018/02/17/brodie-croft-stamps-authority-on-storms-halfback-spot/|title=Brodie Croft stamps authority on Storm's halfback spot|first=Andrew|last=Marmont|website=|date=16 February 2018|access-date=20 February 2018}}</ref> He played in the [[2018 NRL Grand Final]] against the [[Sydney Roosters]] where Melbourne were beaten 21-6.

After playing the first 21 games of the 2019 season at halfback, Croft was surprisingly dropped from the Melbourne line-up entirely, only playing one more game in round 25 at five-eighth, and not making an appearance throughout the finals.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.foxsports.com.au/nrl/nrl-premiership/teams/storm/not-dropped-just-rested-mystery-surrounds-storm-halfback-on-eve-of-finals/news-story/0f8073dd824fabdd263e283323be3887|access-date=6 January 2020|title=Not dropped, just 'rested': Mystery surrounds Storm halfback on eve of finals|date=26 August 2019}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|url=https://wwos.nine.com.au/nrl/croft-gets-another-chance-in-storm-halves/0641a94d-1f31-403b-aaf0-338da241acfc|access-date=6 January 2020|title=Axed Storm star to get last-gasp finals lifeline}}</ref>  In order to remain under the salary cap for 2020, Melbourne elected to release Croft from his contract, officially doing so on 29 November.  That same day, he signed a three-year contract with the [[Brisbane Broncos]].<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.melbournestorm.com.au/news/20192/11/29/an-update-on-brodie-croft-and-curtis-scott/|access-date=6 January 2020|title=An update on Brodie Croft and Curtis Scott|date=28 November 2019}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.broncos.com.au/news/2019/11/29/croft-locks-in-with-broncos-for-three-years/|access-date=6 January 2020|title=Croft locks in with the Broncos for three years|date=29 November 2019}}</ref>

===2020–2021: Brisbane Broncos===
Croft endured a difficult first year in Brisbane and played 14 games for the club as they finished last on the table and claimed the wooden spoon.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.nrl.com/news/2020/09/24/brisbane-get-wooden-spoon-as-cowboys-finish-on-a-high/|title=Brisbane get wooden spoon as North Queensland finish on a high|website=www.nrl.com|date=24 September 2020}}</ref>

In round 20 of the [[2021 NRL season]], Croft scored his first try of the season in a 37-18 thrashing of arch rivals [[North Queensland Cowboys|North Queensland]].  On 9 August, Croft signed a two-year deal to join British side [[Salford Red Devils|Salford]].<ref>{{cite news|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/58144094|title=Salford Red Devils to sign Brisbane Broncos half-back for 2022|work=BBC Sport}}</ref>

===2022–2023: Salford Red Devils===
On 9 August 2021, [[Salford Red Devils]] announced the signing of Brodie Croft from the Brisbane Broncos on a two-year deal. Upon signing for the club, Director of Rugby and Operations [[Ian Blease]] said "Brodie coming to the Red Devils is up there with any signing in recent times for this club. It has taken a long period of time to get this deal done but I am so pleased to get a player of Brodie's quality and class officially across the line. Brodie can come to the Red Devils and enjoy his rugby league again, he is still only a young halfback and after speaking with him over the last few weeks he has the determination to come to the club and push us on with our ambition, it will be tremendous to see him don the Red Devils shirt next season and beyond."{{cn|date=September 2024}}

In Round 1 of the 2022 Super League season, Croft made his club debut for Salford in their 26-16 victory over [[Castleford Tigers|Castleford]].<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.salfordreddevils.net/match-report-castleford-tigers-16-26-salford-red-devils-friday-11th-feb-betfred-super-league-round-1/|title=Match Report Castleford v Salford|website=www.salfordreddevils.net|date=11 February 2022 }}</ref>
Croft played almost every game for Salford in the 2022 Super League season and following a remarkable comeback win away to [[Warrington Wolves|Warrington]] in July 2022, Crofts form prompted [[Salford Red Devils|Salford]] head coach [[Paul Rowley]] to label Croft as the 'best half-back in Super League'. Croft guided Salford to play-off finish and was instrumental in securing the teams preliminary finals spot after a comprehensive victory away at [[Huddersfield Giants|Huddersfield]]. Due to a failed head injury assessment in the Huddersfield game, Croft missed the clubs semi-final defeat to [[St Helens RFC]] due to concussion protocols. Croft finished the regular season with 32 try contributions (25 assists and 7 tries). Croft made over 2,700 running metres with 83 tackle busts and 23 clean breaks. On 20 September 2022, Croft was named [[Man of Steel Awards|Man of Steel]] after being voted as the seasons best player.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.totalrl.com/salford-red-devils-brodie-croft-wins-man-of-steel-award/|title=Salford Red Devils Brodie Croft Wins Man Of Steel Award|website=www.totalrl.com|date=20 September 2022 }}</ref>
In February 2023, Croft signed a seven-year deal to remain at Salford until the end of the 2030 Super League season.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.examinerlive.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/brodie-croft-contract-salford-explained-26181881/|title=Brodie Croft's record Salford Red Devils contract explained - wages, release clause, salary cap|website=www.examinerlive.co.uk}}</ref>
In the 2023 Super League season, Croft played 25 matches for Salford as the club finished 7th on the table and missed the playoffs.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.salfordnow.co.uk/2023/10/02/salford-red-devils-2023-season-in-review/|title=Salford Red Devils' 2023 season in review|website=www.salfordnow.co.uk|date=2 October 2023 }}</ref>

===2023–present: Leeds===
On 18 October 2023, Croft signed a three-year deal to join [[Leeds Rhinos|Leeds]] ahead of the 2024 Super League season.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/67143663|title=Brodie Croft & Andy Ackers: Leeds Rhinos sign Salford Red Devils pair on three-year deals|website=BBC Sport|date=18 Oct 2023}}</ref>
In round 1 of the 2024 Super League season, Croft made his club debut for Leeds against his former team Salford. Leeds would win the match 22-16.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/68304724|title=Super League: Leeds 22-16 Salford - Two tries for Ash Handley as Rhinos outlast Red Devils|website=www.bbc.co.uk}}</ref>
Croft played 25 matches for Leeds in the 2024 Super League which saw the club finish 8th on the table.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.yorkshireeveningpost.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/from-310-to-810-peter-smiths-leeds-rhinos-player-ratings-for-2024-season-4799226/|title=Leeds Rhinos player ratings from the 2024 Super League season|website=www.yorkshireeveningpost.co.uk}}</ref>
Croft played 25 games for Leeds in the 2025 Super League season which saw the club finish 4th on the table. Croft played in their elimination playoff match loss against St Helens.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/articles/ce86r25lrxno/|title=Saints snatch stunning play-off victory at Leeds|website=www.bbc.co.uk}}</ref>
